dag_desc:
Lock the seven-field NodeSpec as the open-source canon today. Every cluster gates on the d-init-decision ratification receipt; every downstream gate is data-flow (consumes ↔ produces) including ratifications.

SCENARIO · given a fresh repo with no .roadmap/, when an outsider follows docs/SETUP.md → reads the README → writes their first spec from examples/hello.spec.json → runs roadmap make / orient / advance, then they reach term without ever encountering depends, priority, ambient, provenance, or idempotent — and the skills they install teach roadmap on its own terms, not fleet's.

STANCE · the engine refuses what the skills ban. examples demonstrate what skills describe. migration is silent for head.json (machine state) and hard-refused for SpecIR (hand-authored, learning surface).

RISKS · dag-dependency-resolver rewrite touches frontier detection · schema_version bump cascades through tests · speckit-import drop is large but the user authorized dangling wires as a learning artifact · examples currently demonstrate the antipattern we're banning · MIGRATION.md must exist before v-reject-depends-field can point at it.

CLUSTERS · init (ratification) · e-strip-* (engine field-cut) · e-add-receipt-validator-* (receipt-as-validator promotion) · r-* (resolver rewrite) · v-* (compile enforcement) · k-* (speckit drop) · s-* (skill scrub + augment + in-repo) · d-* (docs · examples · migration) · t-* (test sweep) · z-* (public surface verification + survey).

INSTALLATION POLICY · prose, not script. SETUP.md is LLM-runnable prose by design. Verification artifact is z-verify-public-surface, which compiles the canonical examples and asserts the public docs render — not a scripted install dry-run. Divergent adopter envs make scripted installs brittle; prose-with-LLM-execution is more honest.

CONVERGENCE POLICY · agents iterate by default. Endogenous failures (test fails · validator rejects · code doesn't compile · hypothesis falsified · my receipt is wrong shape) → keep head down · /core-loop. Exogenous blocks (creds I cannot provision · hardware I cannot reach · service genuinely down · human decision genuinely required) → surface, name the operator action verbatim, leave a BLOCKED receipt, walk to a parallel-ready spine. Discriminator: can I fix this with the tools I already have? The skill text encodes this in s-augment-roadmap-auto-convergence-stance.

VERSIONING · SpecIR schema_version 1 → 2 (hard-refuse v1 with MIGRATION.md pointer · no auto-translate). protocolVersion 0.3.0 → 0.4.0 (silent forward-migration of head.json that nukes dead fields). 1.0.0 reserved until the seven-field shape survives N rounds of survey data.

tasks.desc:
Ratify the OSS release scope and lock the seven-field schema cut

Produce a ratification receipt that downstream clusters consume. Pin the schema_version bump (1→2), the protocolVersion bump (0.3.0→0.4.0), the v1-spec policy (hard-refuse with MIGRATION.md pointer), the head.json policy (silent forward-migration that strips dead fields), and the speckit policy (stub-with-error). Without this receipt, downstream nodes have no anchor and may re-litigate the cut mid-execution.

Strip dead fields from src/lib/protocol/types.ts so NodeSpec is the canonical seven-field interface

NodeSpec keeps id, desc, produces, consumes, validate, mode?, sidecar?. Every other field on the type goes. Downstream files import from here, so this is the load-bearing source. Receipt at .roadmap/round-1/e-strip-protocol-types.json records files_touched and hit_count_pre.

Drop dead fields from SpecIRTask so the IR matches the canonical schema

SpecIRTask shrinks to {id, desc, produces, consumes, validate, mode?, sidecar?}. The compileIR / irTasksToParsed pipeline updates accordingly. Receipt enumerates every dropped field.

Drop dead-field validators from src/lib/schemas.ts and bump SpecIR schema_version literal to 2

The zod specIRTask schema becomes strict-seven-field. schema_version literal moves from z.literal(1) to z.literal(2). v1 specs now produce a structured zod error that the make CLI surfaces with a MIGRATION.md pointer.

Drop ambient and idempotent from NodeMeta in src/runtime/meta.ts

NodeMeta becomes a thin envelope: validate + mode. fullNode() and ManagedGraph stop carrying dead-field defaults.

Stop reading ambient in brief generation

brief-slice.ts and brief.ts and runtime/brief.ts no longer surface an ambient[] field. Sidecar.ambient (when present) becomes the only ambient-style channel and travels via the brief's sidecar pass-through.

Stop emitting ambient and idempotent on plan-mode children

The expansion proposal generator stops authoring dead fields onto runtime-decomposed children. Plan-mode nodes produce children that match the canonical schema.

Drop dead fields from dag insert and modify command surfaces

roadmap dag insert and roadmap dag modify stop accepting depends/idempotent on the --node payload. The CLI help reflects the seven-field shape.

Drop dead fields from spec normalization in roadmap make

The whitelist in make.ts loses priority and depends and deps. Normalization stops back-filling priority by index. The init-node auto-completion stops reading idempotent.

Bump protocolVersion to 0.4.0 and register the dead-field-strip migration for existing head.json

CURRENT_PROTOCOL_VERSION moves to 0.4.0. A migrations['0.3.0->0.4.0'] entry walks every node, deletes priority/depends/ambient/provenance/idempotent in place, and stamps the new version. checkCompatibility surfaces this as needsMigration: true.

Add receipt as a first-class validator type so receipt shape is engine-enforced

The ValidationRule discriminated union grows a new variant: { type: 'receipt', target: <path>, schema: <jsonschema-inline-or-path-ref> }. Zod and TypeScript types update. The runtime handler comes in e-implement-receipt-validation; the brief integration comes in e-brief-pulls-receipt-schema. This is the schema scaffold that closes the brief-receipt-validator drift permanently — instead of brief authors copying receipt shapes by hand and validators probing fields independently, the validator IS the source of truth and the brief is generated from it.

Implement runtime validation for the receipt validator type

When advance encounters a receipt validator, the engine reads the JSON file at target, validates against the inline or referenced JSON schema, and surfaces structured errors on mismatch. Failure messages name the field path and the expected vs actual shape. This is the engine-side half of the brief-receipt-validator triangle fix: the validator becomes load-bearing on receipt shape, not just receipt presence.

Brief generator pulls receipt schemas verbatim into worker briefs under RECEIPT SHAPE

When building a brief for a node, the generator scans validate[] for receipt-type validators and inlines each schema under a clearly-marked RECEIPT SHAPE block. Workers and validators read the same JSON. Authoring drift becomes structurally impossible because there is nothing for the brief author to drift from. This is the worker-side half of the triangle fix.

Rewrite dag-dependency-resolver to derive ordering exclusively from consumes ↔ produces

The resolver stops reading depends. It builds the predecessor graph by matching every consumes path against every node's produces. If a consumes path doesn't trace to a producer, the resolver throws a structured error. This is the heart of the cut.

Switch parallelOrder and frontier detection to read consumes/produces topology only

src/core/order.ts and any caller of the resolver stops passing depends. Tests for parallelOrder build their fixtures from consumes wiring. Frontier nodes are exactly those whose every consumes-path resolves to a completed produce.

Author docs/MIGRATION.md walking adopters from v1 specs to v2

One page. Two worked examples: depends → consumes-via-ratification (init produces .roadmap/init.json, children consume it); priority → delete (priority is vestigial, the ordering you wanted is in consumes). The error message from v-reject-depends-field points at this doc.

validate.command: grep -q 'ratification' docs/MIGRATION.md && grep -q 'depends' docs/MIGRATION.md && grep -q 'priority' docs/MIGRATION.md

tasks.desc:
roadmap make rejects any spec that contains a depends field with a clear MIGRATION.md pointer

The error includes the offending node id, names depends as removed-in-v2, and links docs/MIGRATION.md. This is the structural enforcement of the new authoring rule. Adopters cannot silently carry old specs forward.

validate.command: echo '{"schema_version":2,"engine":{"name":"test","version":null,"config_hash":null},"dag_id":"test","metadata":{"generated":"x","compile_hash":"x"},"inputs":[],"tasks":[{"id":"a","desc":"x","produces":[],"consumes":[],"depends":[],"validate":[]}]}' > /tmp/test-rej.json && ! pnpm dlx tsx src/cli.ts make /tmp/test-rej.json --note test --dry-run 2>&1 | grep -qi MIGRATION

tasks.desc:
Compile rejects non-init nodes with empty consumes — the gate-without-data-flow check

If a node has no incoming consumes path and is not the graph init, the compiler errors. Authors get a clear pointer: 'every gate is consumes-of-an-upstream-produces; create a ratification receipt or wire a real artifact.'

Compile rejects any consumes path not reachable from init via produces chains

Reachability check: every consumes path on every node must trace through some produce chain back to init. Catches typos, dangling references, accidentally-orphaned nodes.

Replace src/lib/intake/speckit-import.ts with a stub that errors on use, pointing at MIGRATION.md

spec-kit support is dropped for the OSS release. Dangling wires in the codebase are intentional learning material — the stub explains why and points at the migration doc. Anyone authoring SpecIR directly is unaffected.

Remove the speckit branch from intake routing in src/lib/intake/intake.ts

intake.ts no longer dispatches to speckit-import on engine: spec-kit. Specs declaring engine: spec-kit produce a clear unsupported-engine error.

Replace fleet-specific examples in roadmap-spec with generic equivalents

QMetaObject / Qt headers / FnPgRov disappear from the title section. Replacement examples use generic engineering vocabulary: 'Compile the API schema from the OpenAPI definition,' 'Verify the dashboard page renders against the design spec,' etc. Skill stays content-equivalent.

Replace fleet-specific worked examples in roadmap-orient with generic ones

emit-camera / sonar / keel-relay / stratum / cycle-8 disappear. The fleet/lane idiom (multiple repos, fleet.json) stays — that's a real roadmap concept. Concrete node names become generic (build-api, deploy-frontend).

Strip the §-anchor reference and the synchronous Griffin / stratum push example from roadmap-auto

The '/fleet-doctrine §Sidecar-as-ambient-context' depth pointer points at a skill that doesn't ship — remove. The 'synchronous Griffin · stratum push' commit example becomes a generic 'human review · CI publish' example.

Audit roadmap-term for any fleet contamination and clean if found

Probe shows zero hits today, but the audit itself is the artifact: a receipt that explicitly records 'no fleet-specific references found' or lists what was changed.

Add the endogenous-vs-exogenous convergence-stance section to roadmap-auto

The default agent stance is heads-down iteration; surfacing is the exception, reserved for genuinely exogenous blocks. Endogenous failures (test fails, validator rejects, schema mismatch, hypothesis falsified, code doesn't compile) → keep head down · iterate · /core-loop on RED. Exogenous blocks (credentials I cannot provision · hardware I cannot reach · service genuinely down · human decision genuinely required · disk full on a remote I do not control) → surface, name the operator action verbatim, leave a BLOCKED receipt as a resume handle, walk to a parallel-ready spine. The discriminator: can I fix this with the tools I already have? Yes → iterate. No → surface. Anti-pattern called out explicitly: throwing hands up before iterating; 'I tried once and it failed' is not a blocker.

Create skills/ at the repo root and check in the cleaned skill files alongside the engine

skills/{roadmap-spec,roadmap-orient,roadmap-auto,roadmap-term}/SKILL.md present in tree. SETUP.md will reference them. Adopters install by copying skills/ → ~/.claude/skills/ (or the equivalent for their agent stack).

validate.command: test -f skills/roadmap-spec/SKILL.md && test -f skills/roadmap-orient/SKILL.md && test -f skills/roadmap-auto/SKILL.md && test -f skills/roadmap-term/SKILL.md

tasks.desc:
Rewrite examples/hello.spec.json in the seven-field shape with a ratification-receipt example

The canonical first-spec demonstrates init→build→term where init produces .roadmap/init.json (the ratification receipt) and build consumes it. No deps. Plain-English titles in every desc. This is the example adopters copy on day one.

Rewrite examples/parallel-build.spec.json so fan-out is expressed via consumes ↔ produces only

build-a and build-b consume the init ratification receipt. integrate consumes the produces of build-a and build-b — no deps anywhere. Demonstrates parallel batches the v2 way.

Update README.md to match the v2 schema and remove deps and idempotent from code samples

The headline JSON example uses the new shape. The TypeScript code sample at line 105 stops using idempotent. The schema explanation table reflects seven fields. README is what adopters read first; it must be self-consistent end-to-end.

Update docs/SETUP.md with a skills-install step pointing at the in-repo skills/ directory

The Claude Code TL;DR adds a step: 'copy skills/* into ~/.claude/skills/ to enable the roadmap-spec, roadmap-orient, roadmap-auto, and roadmap-term skills.' Other-agent-stack TL;DR gets the equivalent.

Replace tests/node-runner/spec-migrate.test.ts with coverage for the 0.3.0 → 0.4.0 dead-field-strip migration

Old migrations (0.1.0→0.2.0, 0.2.0→0.3.0) are gone; their tests go too. New test asserts that head.json with priority/depends/ambient/provenance/idempotent fields gets cleanly stripped after migrateDAG to 0.4.0.

Update test SpecIR fixtures throughout tests/ to drop dead fields and use the seven-field shape

Plan-mode: the actual file list emerges at runtime from grep. Each affected fixture gets its priority/depends/ambient/provenance/idempotent fields removed and its consumes wiring reviewed. ~10 test files touched per the probe.

Add tests covering depends rejection, empty-consumes rejection, consumes-reach-init validation, and receipt-validator runtime

tests/cli-intake-enforcement.test.ts gains scenarios: spec-with-depends rejected with MIGRATION.md pointer · non-init-with-empty-consumes rejected · dangling-consumes-path rejected · receipt-validator success on matching shape · receipt-validator failure on shape drift with field-path-named error. Each test asserts the error message shape, not just the failure.

Run pnpm test and confirm green across all 66 test files

The whole suite passes after the cut, the migrations, and the fixture sweep. Receipt records pass count and runtime.

Verify the public artifacts the adopter actually touches

Installation is prose, not script — SETUP.md is LLM-runnable prose by design and divergent envs make scripted installs brittle. What we CAN verify is that every artifact the prose points at compiles and renders correctly: examples/hello.spec.json and examples/parallel-build.spec.json compile under v2; skills/{spec,orient,auto,term}/SKILL.md exist and are non-trivial; docs/MIGRATION.md contains the expected sections; README.md has no v1 residue. Receipt records the per-artifact verdict.

validate.command: test $(wc -l < skills/roadmap-spec/SKILL.md) -gt 100 && test $(wc -l < skills/roadmap-orient/SKILL.md) -gt 50 && test $(wc -l < skills/roadmap-auto/SKILL.md) -gt 50 && test $(wc -l < skills/roadmap-term/SKILL.md) -gt 50
grep -q ratification docs/MIGRATION.md && grep -q depends docs/MIGRATION.md && grep -q priority docs/MIGRATION.md

tasks.desc:
Append the author-seat survey row for THIS spec to .roadmap/spec-survey.jsonl

The survey instrument from the roadmap-spec skill applies to its own bootstrap. Author seat — five questions — answered before this spec compiles. Demonstrates the survey is real, lives in-repo, and travels with the OSS release as the falsification harness.

Ratify the OSS release: every must-have ships, every example compiles, every test green, MIGRATION.md present, protocolVersion 0.4.0, receipt validator type live, convergence stance landed in roadmap-auto

Terminal validators run end-to-end against the public surface. If any of them fail, the release is not ready. If they all pass, roadmap is open-source as of today.

validate.command: test -f docs/MIGRATION.md && test -f skills/roadmap-spec/SKILL.md && test -f skills/roadmap-orient/SKILL.md && test -f skills/roadmap-auto/SKILL.md && test -f skills/roadmap-term/SKILL.md
